title: Remove all loading states for iframe tabs

requirements.summary: Remove all loading spinners, overlays, and state indicators from iframe tab browsing so that iframes appear immediately with no loading UI
acceptanceCriteria: Opening any URL in a proxy iframe tab shows no loading spinner or overlay at any point.
The tab bar shows no loading indicator on any tab while a site is loading.
The iframe is rendered immediately when a URL is set, without waiting for an onLoad event to hide a spinner.
No loading state (isLoading, loading, etc.) is used to conditionally render or hide the iframe content.
The error state (all proxies failed) is still shown if all proxy attempts fail — only the loading UI is removed, not error handling.

file_operations.description: Remove the loading indicator display from individual tabs. Remove any conditional rendering or styling that shows a loading spinner, progress bar, or loading state icon on tabs while their content is loading.

Remove the loading overlay, spinner, and any conditional rendering that hides the iframe until the onLoad event fires. Render the iframe immediately when a URL is set, regardless of loading state. Preserve the error state UI that displays when all proxy attempts fail.

Remove the isLoading or loading state tracking for tabs entirely, or stop using it to gate iframe rendering. If the loading state is still needed internally for proxy fallback logic, ensure it does not propagate to any UI components or affect rendering decisions.
